Patch Set 5:

It looks the client RPM is installed on tests clients. The server side utilities is absent.

Patch Set 5:

This patch looks quite similar to http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/10510/

One comment on lstclient:

I'm hesitant about removing lstclient. It's the only tool to test userspace lnet, while functionality of ptlctl can be found elsewhere.
Although no one uses userspace lnet for now, I'd still tend to keep it for a while.
If you'd insist to remove it, then please make sure to remove references to it in the Lustre OP manual and man(8) lst.

Patch Set 7:

(2 comments)

The contents of a package should not depend on configure. I know that they do but they shouldn't.

Not that I care deeply but if we delete lustre_config then shouldn't we also delete all of lustre/scripts/lc_*? Can these be used without lustre_config?

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

Not sure about lustre_config. I assume it just a part of utilities  which converters configuration in/from CSV and can be removed as not used.

parents.subject: LU-5396 lov: add sparse annotation __user wherever needed

commit.subject: LU-5104 build: utilities cleanup
commit.message:
LU-5104 build: utilities cleanup

- remove unused utilities: lustre_createcsv, lustre_config, lustre_up14
- move req_layout, loadgen, wirecheck and wiretest to tests RPM
- add dependency tests from utils (multiop from liblustreapi)
- make corrent mapping for configure parameters
  "--enable-*/--disable-*" to "--with/--without *"
- fix find command for empty results
- fix utils building (add ifdef UTILS and fix .spec file)
- don't install few server scripts and utils for client build

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
